differential_diagnoses: Vitamin A deficiency (Xerophthalmic keratomalacia and optic neuropathy)
Nutritional optic neuropathy (Vitamin B12, folate, copper deficiency)
Toxic optic neuropathy (e.g., heavy metal exposure or medication)
next_steps: Obtain serum levels of Vitamin A (retinol), Vitamin E, Vitamin D, Vitamin B12, folate, copper, and zinc
Consult Ophthalmology for comprehensive evaluation of Bitot's spots/corneal xerosis
Initiate emergent supplementation of Vitamin A (palmitate) and other identified nutritional deficiencies
Perform genetic testing for Leber hereditary optic neuropathy (LHON) and OPA1 mutations
Consult Nutrition/Dietetics for assessment of selective eating disorder and implementation of oral/parenteral supplementation
Consult Neurology for further assessment of the CNS imaging findings (calvarial thickening)
Engage low-vision rehabilitation and social services support

differential_diagnoses: Combined nutritional optic neuropathy (B12, folate, and copper deficiency)
Metabolic bone disease secondary to multiple nutritional deficiencies (Vitamin D, Calcium)
next_steps: Obtain serum levels of vitamins A, C, D, E, B12, folate, copper, zinc, and metabolic panel including calcium, phosphorus, alkaline phosphatase, and PTH
Initiate urgent vitamin A supplementation under ophthalmology guidance
Urgent ophthalmology evaluation to document and monitor corneal and conjunctival findings
Nutrition/Dietetics consultation for management of severe selective eating and ARFID
Physical assessment for signs of scurvy (perifollicular hemorrhages, corkscrew hairs, gingival health)
Physical medicine and rehabilitation or neurology consultation for physical exam regarding the calvarial thickening and motor findings
Referral for low-vision rehabilitation and social work support
